I read the prompt and the 23-turn proposal log. The prompt fixes the tame-format presentation with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, and constants modulo M=85667662080. The only still-live proposal in the prior log is the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9â€“23; the earlier candidates were refuted by explicit finite tame/index-two tests.

The surviving candidate is still variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with Ï€=Ï€2 and Î·=2Ï€,r=xÏƒâˆ’Ï€xÏƒÏ€xâˆ’1(xÏ„âˆ’Î·)Ï€ ((yÏ„)Ï€)Ïƒyâˆ’1,where Ï€â‰¡40491355905,âˆ’Ï€â‰¡45176306175,âˆ’Î·=âˆ’2Ï€ â‰¡50932630783(modM).Correction to the prior unramified-block algebra There is a small but important algebraic correction to the recent turns. In the unramified quotient of degree f=2a m, write sâ‰¡Ï€(modf),sâ‰¡1(mod2a),sâ‰¡0(modm).In the trivial tame-inertia block the mod-2 linear relation is(1+Tâˆ’s)X+(1+T)Y=0.

When f is even, s is odd, so 1+Tâˆ£1+Tâˆ’s.

Therefore the correct elimination is

Y=Q(T)X+YË‰,Q(T)=1+T1+Tâˆ’s,not the reciprocal. The nonsingularity argument survives unchanged, because (1+Î¶)(1+Q(Î¶)+Q(Î¶âˆ’1))=Î¶âˆ’s+Î¶s+1.

Thus a zero would force Î¶2s+1=1, and 2s+1 is coprime to f. So the prior conclusion about the trivial block is still right, but any final proof should use this corrected quotient.

New check: the f=3 unramified quotient detects the S1/3term

Turn 23 correctly observed that for a general unramified quotient one must writeÏƒÏ€=ÏƒsSq,S=Ïƒf,q=f1âˆ’sâˆˆZ2.For odd f, s=0, so q=1/f. The first nontrivial case is f=3, whereÏƒÏ€=S1/3.I pushed this case through an exact symbolic Reidemeisterâ€“Schreier calculation. With Ï„=1, the live relation becomesr=xÏƒâˆ’Ï€xÏƒÏ€ [Ïƒ,y].

For the unramified cubic quotient, take generatorsxi=ÏƒixÏƒâˆ’i,yi=ÏƒiyÏƒâˆ’i,S=Ïƒ3.

The kernel relators arer0=x0Sâˆ’qx0Sqy1y0âˆ’1,r1=x1Sâˆ’qx1Sqy2y1âˆ’1,r2=x2Sâˆ’qx2Sq+1y0Sâˆ’1y2âˆ’1,where q=1/3. Their abelianization gives2Xi+Yi+1âˆ’Yi=0,iâˆˆZ/3,soUabâ‰…Z24âŠ•C2,as expected for the maximal pro-2 Galois group of an unramified cubic 2-adic field.

Now take the sign/orientation characterÎº(Xi)=Îº(Yi)=1,Îº(S)=0.Let V=kerÎº. A symbolic Reidemeisterâ€“Schreier abelianization of V, keeping q as a formal 2-adic parameter, gives a 6Ã—13 relation matrix whose nonzero maximal minors are all Â±2(3q+1).

Hence the unique 2-primary torsion factor of Vab has valuationv2 (2(3q+1))=1+v2 (3q+1).

For the actual candidate,q=31,3q+1=2,soVabâ‰…Z27âŠ•C4.That is exactly what the sign kernel should have: the corresponding field is the unramified cubic field with i adjoined, of degree 6, so its pro-2 local class field abelianization has free rank 6+1=7 and Î¼4 -torsion.

This test also shows that the S1/3 term is not cosmetic. If one naively suppressed it by taking q=0, the sign kernel would have only C2 -torsion. If one incorrectly used q=1, the sign kernel would have C8 -torsion. Thus q=1/3 is forced already by the cyclotomic orientation in the unramified cubic quotient.I also enumerated the next quadratic layer for this f=3 sign kernel, using qâ‰¡11(mod32), the reduction of 1/3 sufficient to see C8 -torsion. The 255 index-two subgroups of V have the distribution 254Â subgroupsÂ withÂ C4 -torsion,1Â subgroupÂ withÂ C8 -torsion,and all have free rank 13. This matches the expected unique Î¼8 -branch above the field already containing Î¼4.

So Turn 23â€™s suggestion that f=3 should detect the S1/3 term was exactly right. The live candidate passes that test, and wrong q-values fail it sharply.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I corrected the trivial-block elimination to

Q=(1+Tâˆ’s)/(1+T).In the unramified cubic quotient, the sign-kernel abelianization has torsion factor 2(3q+1). For the candidateâ€™s required q=1/3, this is C4; wrong choices q=0 or q=1 give the wrong cyclotomic torsion. The next layer over the sign kernel has the expected 254Ã—C4, 1Ã—C8 distribution.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the full profinite presentation. The remaining issue is the dyadic formation/rigidity theorem: one must identify the full filtered Frobenius action, with all Sq -terms and tame-inertia corrections, with arithmetic Frobenius over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: Generalize the f=3 Smith calculation. The likely symbolic target for odd unramified f is a sign-kernel torsion factor 2(fq+1), which would force q=1/f and give C4 uniformly. Then extend the same calculation to even f=2a m with sî€ =0.[BREAKTHROUGH]
